what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG outline icon depicting a timer or stopwatch, using currentColor strokes and no fill.
observations: Draws the icon with two paths for the top bar and timer hand, one circle for the body, and one path for the side control.
Uses fill="none" and stroke="currentColor" for theme-adaptive coloring.
Sets stroke-width to 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
standouts: Timer shape is built from simple geometric primitives.
Color is inherited from surrounding text or CSS via currentColor.
conventions: Uses inline SVG attributes rather than external styling.
Uses semantic filename alignment with the depicted timer shape.
Does not include title, desc, role, or aria attributes in the SVG itself.
concerns: No embedded accessibility label or descriptive metadata if used standalone.
notes: A minimal currentColor outline timer icon suitable for reuse in a design system.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component?
Is stroke width 1.5 the expected icon style for this icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a timer icon, and the SVG content depicts a timer shape, so the apparent purpose matches the actual content.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable timer glyph that can inherit color from its UI context while staying visually consistent with an outlined icon set.
contribution: Contributes a compact timer icon substrate with currentColor styling, rounded stroke treatment, and simple 24x24 geometry for a fused design system to retain as a reusable time-related visual token.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG outline timer icon composed of simple paths and a circle with rounded stroke styling.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing timer or stopwatch functionality as a reusable themed icon asset.
